title: Executive Order 11512—Planning, Acquisition, and Management of Federal Space

flags.justification: Sec. 2(b) provides a genuine appeal mechanism: an affected agency head disagreeing with the Administrator's determination may request review by the President through the Director of the Bureau of the Budget within thirty days.
Seven specific enumerated policy factors (Sec. 2(a)) guide decisions, combined with the appeal mechanism, provide real, checkable structure.
Single coherent purpose: federal space planning and management.
The seven enumerated guiding factors, while requiring judgment, are specific and are backstopped by the presidential appeal mechanism.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing administrative function (federal property management), evergreen by nature; this order itself supersedes a prior order (EO 11035), evidencing periodic revision.
The presidential appeal mechanism is an added check, not a removal of an existing one.
The thirty-day appeal window with Bureau of the Budget review provides a real, if internal-to-government, external check on the Administrator's determinations.
A routine, if comprehensive, administrative property-management framework; no novel authority claim.
Sec. 2(a)(2) requires consultation with the Secretaries of HUD, HEW, and Commerce for site-selection factors — designed coordination, not conflict.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/20 = 0.0%. A well-designed order with a genuine, structured appeal-to-the-President mechanism for agency disagreements.
